title: WildFly Undertow Worker Thread Saturation
dataSources: `index=web` `sourcetype=jboss:server` (`server.log`) Undertow/XNIO worker thread summaries; optional JMX `jmx:jboss` for `undertow` worker metrics
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder; Splunk Add-on for JMX (`Splunk_TA_jmx`); file monitoring for `jboss:server`, structured audit for `wildfly:audit`
spl:
index=web sourcetype="jboss:server"
| regex _raw="(?i)XNIO.*worker"
| rex field=_raw "IO-threads=(?<io_threads>\d+).*worker-threads=(?<worker_current>\d+)/(?<worker_max>\d+)"
| eval w_util=if(worker_max>0, round(100*tonumber(worker_current)/tonumber(worker_max),1), null())
| where w_util >= 90
description: Undertow uses XNIO worker threads for blocking servlet work. When workers are exhausted, latency climbs while CPU may look healthy. Log-derived utilization complements JMX for quick alerting.
value: Protects user-facing latency on WildFly-hosted APIs and portals.
implementation: Forward `$JBOSS_HOME/standalone/log/server.log` via UF. For precision, add `Splunk_TA_jmx` MBeans `undertow` worker. Alert on worker utilization ≥90%.
visualization: Time series for pool/queue metrics, top stats for failures, event timeline for deploys.
grandmaExplanation: We watch WildFly HTTP workers so requests are not stuck waiting for threads.
